message:
perf(raptorq): raise addmul floor to 12KiB and add XOR fast path for tiny slices

Two performance improvements based on Track-E benchmark evidence:

GF256 profile packs (gf256.rs):
- Raise addmul_min_total from 8KiB to 12KiB for SSE2 and AVX2 profile
  packs. Track-E evidence showed fused addmul regressed at the
  4KiB+4KiB sequential path footprint, requiring a higher floor
- Update test expectations to match new 12KiB floor
- Add regression guard test asserting SIMD profile packs keep addmul
  floor above 8KiB (prevents future regressions)

Gaussian elimination (linalg.rs):
- Add row_xor_tiny() inline fast path for slices <= 32 bytes that uses
  a direct XOR loop instead of dispatching through SIMD kernel setup
- Apply fast path in three eliminate_row code paths: RHS-only updates,
  paired tail+RHS updates, and tail-only updates
- Small symbol sizes (common in streaming/real-time scenarios) skip
  kernel dispatch overhead entirely
- Add eliminate_row_pivot_only_with_rhs_one_updates_rhs_only test
  covering the RHS-only XOR path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
